div.head:
No. 116.
Mr. Martinez to Mr. Hitt.

opener.dateline:
Legation of Chili,
Washington, D. C., May 4, 1881.

div.p: Esteemed Sir and Friend: The New York papers are publishing a rumor that it is possible that Chili may establish a protectorate over Peru, because that country cannot form a government of its own, and because many sensible people are asking for such a protectorate.

I am sorry that I cannot call and speak with Mr. Blaine personally on this subject. Nevertheless I desire to inform him, through you, that there is no ground whatever for such rumor; and that, even though all the Peruvians should clamor for a protectorate, Chili would on no account consent to establish one.
